title: Avoid Throwing Generic Exceptions
description: Throwing generic exceptions such as Error, RuntimeException, Throwable, and Exception negatively impacts any code trying to catch these exceptions. It forces consumers to catch exceptions they do not intend to handle, which they then have to re-throw. Hence, it is recommended to throw the most specific exception possible so that it can be handled intentionally by consumers.
recommendation: Throw the most specific exception possible rather than a generic one. This allows exceptions to be handled more intentionally and avoids the need for consumers to catch exceptions they do not intend to.
impact: Throwing a generic exception forces consumers to catch exceptions they do not intend to handle, which can lead to error-prone and difficult maintenance. Legitimate exceptions may be unintentionally silenced and errors may be hidden.

checks.message: Throw the most specific exception possible rather than a generic one. This allows exceptions to be handled more intentionally and avoids the need for consumers to catch exceptions they do not intend to.
